articles.title: Build-A-Bear Workshop at Disneyland's Downtown Disney
articles.summary: This article provides a photo-centric look at the ribbon-cutting ceremony for the Build-A-Bear Workshop at Disneyland's Downtown Disney. It features images of the event, including the Build-A-Bear Employee Kazoo band and special guests, celebrating the opening of the new location. The content appears to be a segment of a larger photo essay or article, indicated by the "Page 6 of 8" navigation.

articles.title: Hasbro launches Power Rangers shorts on YouTube
articles.summary: Hasbro has launched a new short-form YouTube series called "Mighty Morphin Minute" to re-engage younger audiences with the 32-year-old Power Rangers franchise. These shorts feature recompiled episodes from the original series, simplifying the story and focusing on action scenes. This initiative is part of a broader strategy, which includes a new "Re-Ignition" toy line from Playmates, an AI-upscaled original series on Netflix, and a new live-action show concept for Disney+.

articles.title: Disney’s Power Players Dominate the 2025 Upfront as Storytelling and Innovation Stand Front and Center
articles.summary: The Walt Disney Company's 2025 Upfront event highlighted its leadership in sports, streaming, entertainment, and technology, emphasizing its commitment to innovation and storytelling for advertisers. The event showcased upcoming content, including the fourth season of "The Bear," new sports shows, and details on ESPN's direct-to-consumer offering, featuring over 100 celebrity appearances. Disney reinforced its position with 164 million global ad-supported monthly active users and 200,000 hours of premium content.

articles.title: If You Invested $10K In Walt Disney Stock 10 Years Ago, How Much Would You Have Now?
articles.summary: An investor who put $10,000 into Walt Disney stock ten years ago would now have $10,941, representing a 9.41% total return from stock appreciation and dividends. This return significantly lags the S&P 500's 231% total return over the same period. Despite past performance, analysts project a "Buy" rating for Disney with a potential upside of over 13%, driven by strong Q2 2025 earnings and performance in its Entertainment and Experiences segments.

articles.title: Investing in the Next Generation at The Walt Disney Company Japan
articles.summary: The Walt Disney Company Japan emphasizes nurturing its "Next Generation" workforce through resources and growth opportunities. The article highlights three professionals—Hidemi in social media, Daiichiro in brand commercialization, and Saori in merchandising—who have experienced significant career development within the company. Their stories underscore the company's commitment to supportive leadership, challenging projects, and encouraging proactive employee engagement, contributing to both personal and business success.
